RECRUITMENT

Positions Open

The Brent lab is looking for a few people for the protein networks project. This is a pure functional genomics effort. In it, we are using interaction mating to assign function to genes involved in cell proliferation. We are doing this by assaying their interactions with large panels of known baits and preys. In the next few years, we expect to have panels of several thousand members and to be using them to get clues to function of several hundred genes a year. What we learn from this effort will allow us and others to scale up until we can perform such assays on the entire array of genes and allelic variants from human and other genomes.

The nature of this effort will require several people whose skills and personalities are at least slightly different from the historical norm for the lab.

1) Organizer of panel effort person. Person will be a postdoctoral fellow or graduate student. Person, working with a technician and other lab members, will be responsible for taking the current panel of ~750 informative proteins, adding proteins to them so that they contain ~3000 proteins, and gradually ramping up the number of proteins we throw against them. Person doing this should be skilled in nucleic acid manipulations (or at least be willing to get very good very fast). Some previous microbiology or genetic training a plus. Person should have an extremely broad general knowledge of biology. Person should have a personality that will allow him/her to take pride in making the panels as informative as possible, to take pride in setting up an operation that generates large numbers of standardized, high quality, fusion genes, and to take pride in doing running the interaction matings and generating lots and lots of lovely data. Person should takes craftsmanlike joy in the work each week, and must be able to work at that level week after week because they are focussed on the goal. Person would typically be a postdoctoral fellow with a biology background but might be a graduate student or an emigrant from an experimental natural science or engineering background.

2) Database person. Person will be in charge of the protein connections database, which will store and make available to other researchers information on the contacts that proteins make with one another. To this end person will help design, write, and maintain the database. Person will also have primary responsibility for the platform that the database runs on (probably a Sun UltraSparc Station). Person will work closely with members of the lab generating this data. Person will also work closely with Dr. Larry Lok, an algorithim/mathmatics consultant, and Dr. J. Michael Cherry, an informatics consultant. Person may also contribute to our effort to extract biololgical information from this data. Familiarity with Unix and C a must, familiarity with SQL and one or more common databases highly valued. Database will be implemented in Illustra. I do not have a strong preconception of who this person will be. Person could be a PhD biologist or graduate student with substantial informatics experience. Alternatively, person could be a PhD or graduate student in Mathematics, Computer Science, or other natural science with a strong informatics background and desire to learn how to make fundamental contributions to the understanding of gene function. Person could also be a recently graduated undergraduate who desired on the job training in functional genomics/bioinformatics. In all cases salary would be appropriate.
